CHAIR’S STATEMENT
Background
As longer standing shareholders will be aware from previous communications, the sale in November 2024 of abrdn Property Holdings Limited (aPH) resulted in the disposal of the investment property portfolio barring one asset.
This final holding is the land in the Cairngorms known as Far Ralia, which was originally acquired as part of the Company’s Net Zero Carbon target and is currently being marketed for sale.
There is an update on progress within the Investment Manager’s report.
Following a sale of Far Ralia, and in line with the shareholder vote in May 2024, it is the Board’s intention to progress with a liquidation of the Company and return the proceeds to shareholders.
Review of 2025
As outlined in the 2025 Interim Report & Accounts, the Board and Investment Manager continue to work towards a formal liquidation of the Company, and it remains their sole focus to maximise returns to shareholders and liquidate the Company as soon as practically possible.
During the year and following agreement of the completion accounts in relation to the sale of aPH, the Company returned approximately £15 million to shareholders by way of a Return of Capital (via redeemable bonus shares) and a Final Property Income Distribution.
The Board, in discussion with the Investment Manager, is satisfied that following this distribution the Company retains a prudent level of funds to cover its outgoings for a sufficient period of time to facilitate a liquidation.
The Board remain cognisant that the Company no longer has any income producing assets (excluding interest on cash holdings) and the costs of running the Company are now eroding shareholder funds by approximately £600,000 per annum (net of interest on cash holdings) based on current projections.
The Board and Investment Manager are, therefore, focused on minimising expenditure.
In line with this, the Board have taken the decision to reduce their annual Director’s fees by 10% from 1 April 2026.
Additionally, during the year the Board decided to change the Company’s auditor from Deloitte LLP to Grant Thornton Limited as the latter represented better value for money. This forms part of an ongoing exercise whereby the costs of all the Company's suppliers are being scrutinised in efforts to reduce these where possible.
Potential Delisting
As part of these cost saving efforts, the Board considered the potential for the Company to delist from the London Stock Exchange.
Whilst offering an attractive reduction in costs, it was noted that this could have a material impact on some shareholders.
In

latest.title: Change of Auditor
latest.content:
Today 07:00
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited - Change of Auditor
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited - Change of Auditor
PR Newswire
LONDON, United Kingdom, February 23
ABRDN PROPERTY INCOME TRUST LIMITED Legal Entity Identifier: 549300HHFBWZRKC7RW84
23 February 2026
CHANGE OF AUDITOR
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited (the “Company”) is pleased to announce the appointment of Grant Thornton Limited as the Company's external auditor.
Grant Thornton Limited will conduct the audit of the Company's financial statements for the financial year ending 31 December 2025.
The appointment has been approved by the Board and follows the resignation of Deloitte LLP, the Company's former auditor. Deloitte LLP has provided a statement to the Company confirming that there are no circumstances connected with the change of auditor that need to be brought to the attention of the Company's members or creditors under section 273(2) of The Companies (Guernsey) Law, 2008 (as amended).
The reappointment of Grant Thornton Limited as external auditor will be subject to the approval of shareholders at the 2026 Annual General Meeting.
The Board would like to thank Deloitte LLP for their service and support over the years.
For further information, please contact:
The Company SecretaryNorthern Trust International Fund Administration Services (Guernsey) Limited
PO Box 255
Trafalgar Court
Les Banques
St Peter Port
Guernsey
GY1 3QL
Tel: 01481 745001
END

company_30s.what_they_do: API.L is a very small London‑listed entity that has sold its core assets and is now focused on returning the remaining cash to shareholders.  It has no operating business left and is effectively a shell awaiting final liquidation.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Even though the company has no revenue, it is a vehicle for a capital return to its shareholders.  For investors who own the stock, the final payout is the only thing that matters, and the share price is a proxy for how much of that cash is still available.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in the final stages of a statutory winding‑up.  It has already announced a return of 52p per share, and the latest annual report confirms that no further business activity is planned.  The share price is trading at a fraction of a penny, reflecting the near‑zero intrinsic value.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings show a clear path to liquidation: a return of capital announced in November 2025, a final property income distribution, and an annual report in April 2026 that confirms no remaining assets.
The change of auditor and the disclosure of holdings in March 2026 are routine administrative steps that usually accompany a winding‑up process.

The AGM in August 2025 and the interim results in September 2025 were both focused on finalising the sale of assets and preparing for the capital return, signalling that the company is closing out its books.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: The community is largely silent – no one is actively trading or discussing the stock.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There is no bullish chatter.  The few posts that exist simply confirm the capital return and express relief that the process is ending.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point out that the company has sold all of its assets and is handing back cash.  The only risk left is that the final payout could be lower than expected if the asset sale was over‑valued.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from mild confusion in early December to a quiet resignation by the end of the month, as the community realised the stock is effectively a cash‑return vehicle.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – there is no search interest, which is typical for a company that is no longer operating.

near_term_catalysts: The scheduled payment of 52p per share on or about 24 December 2024 is the only concrete event that could move the price.
If the company fails to meet its capital‑return deadline, the share price could drop further to a few pence or even become worthless.
A surprise announcement of an additional asset sale or a new investment deal would be the only way the stock could recover beyond the 52p payout.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for the actual payment date and the volume of shares traded on that day.  A spike in volume accompanied by a price jump would confirm that the payout is happening.  Any mention of a new shareholder agreement or a second liquidation plan would be a red flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the final liquidation phase – the crash has already been completed and the only possible recovery is the capital return.  There is no room for a typical mean‑reversion bounce.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the stock has no business prospects and is only worth the cash that will be returned.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company manages to return the full 52p per share, shareholders will recover that amount.  However, the upside is capped at 52p, and any price above that would be speculative.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has already sold all its assets; the share price is essentially a token of the capital return.  If the payout is delayed or reduced, the stock could drop to zero, leaving investors with nothing.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: The share could become worthless if the liquidation process fails or is delayed.  Reward: The maximum gain is the 52p payout – any price above that would need an unforeseen event, which is highly unlikely.

playbook.if_bullish: Not applicable – there is no realistic upside beyond the capital return.
playbook.if_watching: Monitor the 24 December 2024 payment date and the trading volume that day.  If the share price rises to 52p or above, it indicates the payout is happening; otherwise, expect a further decline.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delay, a reduction in the payout, or a move to a different liquidation structure, consider selling any remaining shares before the price collapses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: API.L is in the final liquidation stage, so the only realistic return is the 52p capital payout; any further price movement is unlikely and carries the risk of the share becoming worthless.
